expectations.text: mentions-extension-setup: The answer explains how to load the extension in Chrome. Expect: mentions chrome://extensions, developer mode, load unpacked, extension/ directory
expectations.evidence: answer.md lines 11-17: "Open Chrome and navigate to chrome://extensions/", "Enable Developer mode (toggle in the top-right corner)", "Click Load unpacked and select the project's extension/ directory: /home/rczx/workspace/rinbarpen/projects/auto-browser/extension/"

expectations.text: mentions-sidepanel: The answer mentions the sidepanel as the operator console. Expect: references sidepanel or sidepanel.html
expectations.evidence: answer.md line 5: "Chrome MV3 sidepanel"; line 132: "Monitor progress in the sidepanel's timeline strip"; line 188: "extension/sidepanel.js + sidepanel.html"; plus full keyboard shortcuts table for the sidepanel at lines 136-144

expectations.text: explains-extension-execution-flow: The answer explains how extension execution differs from service execution. Expect: mentions observe-decide-act cycle, POST /api/tasks/:id/decide, no system mouse, DOM-only actions
expectations.evidence: answer.md does mention the observe-decide-act cycle (lines 131, 150) but does NOT mention POST /api/tasks/:id/decide, no system mouse, or DOM-only actions. Three of four expected indicators are missing.

expectations.text: explains-credential-file-format: The answer shows the credentials.json format. Expect: Shows the JSON structure with sites, username, password fields
expectations.evidence: answer.md lines 50-62 shows complete JSON: {"sites": {"example.com": {"username": "your-username", "password": "your-password"}, ...}}. config.md lines 7-27 also shows the same structure with multiple site entries.

expectations.text: mentions-site-permissions: The answer mentions that the extension requests runtime site permissions. Expect: mentions granting site access or permissions per origin
expectations.evidence: answer.md Step 7 (lines 170-178): "Granting Site Permissions in the Extension", "A permission dialog appears in the sidepanel showing 'Permission required for <origin>'", "Click Grant site access to approve the permission for that origin", "chrome.permissions.request()", "Details -> Site access"

suggestions: Mention the POST /api/tasks/:id/decide endpoint explicitly when explaining the extension execution flow to make the architectural distinction between service-side and extension-side execution clearer.
Note that the extension has no system mouse and performs DOM-only actions, which is a key operational difference from the Playwright-based service execution.
The credential limitation section is a good start on the difference, but it focuses on credentials rather than the broader architectural difference in execution model.
eval_feedback.overall: The answer is thorough on extension setup, credentials configuration, and site permissions. The main gap is in explaining the extension execution flow differences: it mentions the observe-decide-act cycle but omits the specific endpoint (POST /api/tasks/:id/decide), the lack of system mouse, and the DOM-only action model.
